pkg/framework/transport/httptransport.purpose: Generic typed HTTP handler pipeline (decode → operation → encode) shared by every domain httpdriver and api/v3/handlers package — Handler[Request,Response] is the universal adapter between Chi routes and domain service calls. Its encoder/ child declares the pure ResponseEncoder/ErrorEncoder function types that break the import cycle with commonhttp.

patterns.name: NewHandler triple constructor
patterns.description: Always construct via NewHandler(requestDecoder, op, responseEncoder, ...options); never instantiate handler{} directly — defaultHandlerOptions (GenericErrorEncoder) are injected only by newHandler.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs for path-param injection
patterns.description: Bake a Chi URL param into the decoder via NewHandlerWithArgs + .With(arg) at mount time; With() clones the value-receiver handler binding the arg into decodeRequest.

patterns.name: ErrorEncoder chain, first match wins
patterns.description: WithErrorEncoder appends to errorEncoders; ServeHTTP iterates in order and the first returning true short-circuits. GenericErrorEncoder is appended last by defaultHandlerOptions, so custom encoders must be passed as options to NewHandler.

patterns.name: Chain for cross-cutting middleware
patterns.description: handler.Chain(outer, ...others) wraps the operation with operation.Middleware and returns a new handler value; the original is unmodified.

patterns.name: SelfEncodingError escape hatch
patterns.description: Errors implementing SelfEncodingError (EncodeError(ctx, w) bool) bypass the ErrorEncoder chain; reserved for infrastructure error types like models.StatusProblem, not domain errors.
patterns.example: // models.StatusProblem implements SelfEncodingError and calls Respond(w) directly

key_file_guides.role: Core Handler[Req,Resp] interface and internal handler struct; ServeHTTP orchestrates decode→operation→encode; encodeError iterates the chain, then SelfEncodingError, then falls back to a 500 problem+json. defaultHandlerOptions appends GenericErrorEncoder unconditionally.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not pass GenericErrorEncoder again via WithErrorEncoder — it is already appended; a second copy double-encodes.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerWithArgs variant for route-param injection; With() clones the value-receiver handler and swaps decodeRequest; Chain() rebuilds the operation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both With() and Chain() rely on value-receiver copy semantics — adding pointer fields to handler would share state across copies and require explicit deep copy.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerOption interface and all WithX constructors; the errorEncoders slice is order-sensitive (append); resolveErrorHandler returns dummyErrorHandler when none set; AppendOptions helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Custom domain encoders passed after defaultHandlerOptions land after GenericErrorEncoder and never fire — always pass custom encoders as options to NewHandler.

key_file_guides.role: Declares ResponseEncoder[T] and ErrorEncoder pure function types with no logic — imported by both handler.go and commonhttp without a cycle.
key_file_guides.watch_for: An ErrorEncoder must return false if it did not write to ResponseWriter; returning true after a partial write corrupts the response.

anti_patterns: Instantiating handler{} struct directly — bypasses defaultHandlerOptions and loses GenericErrorEncoder
Calling context.Background() inside a RequestDecoder — always use the ctx passed from ServeHTTP
Implementing SelfEncodingError on domain-level errors — reserve it for infrastructure types; domain errors belong in the ErrorEncoder chain
Adding state (mutexes, caches) to HandlerWithArgs or handler — value types cloned by With()/Chain() would race on shared pointer fields
Writing to ResponseWriter inside a RequestDecoder on error — return the error and let the ErrorEncoder chain handle it

decisions.rationale: Enables safe per-request arg injection and middleware wrapping without allocation or locking; the registered handler is immutable after construction.

decisions.decision: Last-resort fallback to 500 lives in handler.go, not in GenericErrorEncoder
decisions.rationale: Keeps the fallback authoritative and unconfigurable — any error not handled by the chain or SelfEncodingError always yields a well-formed 500 problem+json.

decisions.decision: Separate encoder sub-package for the encoder function types
decisions.rationale: Breaks the import cycle between handler.go (which uses both types) and commonhttp (which defines GenericErrorEncoder); encoder has no dependencies so both can import it.

code_examples.scenario: Minimal domain HTTP handler wired into a Chi router
